I Let an AI Agent Touch My Codebase. Then I Built a Human Approval Layer Before It Could Break Anything.
Press enter or click to view image in full size

AI coding agents are exciting until they start changing files without asking you.
That was the exact moment I stopped trusting the “fully automatic” dream.
I was building a small Python workflow where an AI agent could read a task, understand what needed to be changed, and then update files inside a project. At first, it felt magical. I gave it a simple instruction like, “Create a helper function for logging,” and it understood the task quickly. It planned the change, selected the file, and prepared the code.
Then I tested it with a slightly unclear request.
The agent tried to edit the wrong file.
Nothing terrible happened because I was still testing, but that small mistake gave me a very clear lesson: an AI coding agent should not directly control your codebase without a human approval step.
That is where the idea of a Human-in-the-Loop AI Coding Agent in Python became very important for me.
A human-in-the-loop system simply means the AI can think, suggest, and prepare the work, but the final decision stays with a human…
